anchor.snippet: the ratio of the greater to the lesser is between 1 and 10

annotations.label: Numbers within an order of magnitude

anchor.snippet: 1 and 1.02 are within an order of magnitude

annotations.label: Order of magnitude as factor of ten

anchor.snippet: Each division or multiplication by 10 is called an order of magnitude
there is one order of magnitude between 2 and 20
the order of magnitude of a number is the smallest power of 10 used to represent that number
represents a geometric halfway point within the range of possible values
Some use a simpler definition where
If numbers differ by one order of magnitude, one number is about 10 times larger than the other
If values differ by two orders of magnitude, they differ by a factor of about 100
Two numbers of the same order of magnitude have roughly the same scale

anchor.section: Calculating the order of magnitude by truncation
anchor.snippet: the order of magnitude of a number can be defined in terms of the common logarithm

anchor.section: Calculating the order of magnitude by truncation
anchor.snippet: the number 4 000 000 has a logarithm (in base 10) of 6.602; its order of magnitude is 6

anchor.section: Calculating the order of magnitude by truncation
anchor.snippet: the order of magnitude is the number of figures minus one
is an estimate rounded to the nearest power of ten
for a variable between about 3 billion and 30 billion

annotations.label: Rounding to nearest order of magnitude

anchor.snippet: one rounds its logarithm to the nearest integer
requires rounding up to the next power of ten when the multiplier is greater than the square root of ten
the nearest order of magnitude for 1.7 × 10 8 is 8
is sometimes also called a zeroth order approximation

annotations.label: Order of magnitude relative to reference value

anchor.snippet: An order of magnitude is an approximation of the logarithm of a value relative to some contextually understood reference value
the order of magnitude can be understood as the number of digits minus one in the base-10 representation of the value
each increasing level is brighter by a factor of
a level being 5 magnitudes brighter than another
This series of magnitudes forms a logarithmic scale with a base of

anchor.snippet: the order of magnitude is included in the number name in this example
